design_principles
detail
10 intermolecular H-bonds predicted in complex with β-D-glucose
TEM rings ~8.4 Å apart — ideal for all-equatorial carbohydrate
two TEM aromatic surfaces sandwich glucose α-face and β-face axial CH groups
Inversion of any hydroxyl (Gal=C4, Man=C2) disrupts H-bond network → ~100x loss
removing 2-OH = 25x Ka reduction despite no steric clash → H-bond essential
ConA Ka ~500 M⁻¹ for glucose; receptor 2 Ka = 18000 M⁻¹ — 36x stronger
Previous best synthetic lectin Ka ~250 M⁻¹; receptor 2 is ~72x stronger
no change by ¹H NMR after 150°C for 1 hour
no toxicity to HeLa cells at up to 1 mM for 18 hours
